title: hebe rapensis
intro: Hebe rapensis, also known as Rapensis Hebe, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the Cook Islands. It has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Hebe rapensis can be prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments or as a preventative measure.
